title: agrostemma githago subsp. thessalum
intro: Agrostemma githago subsp. thessalum, also known as the corn cockle, is a plant species within the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its bright pink to red flowers and distinctive appearance. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Agrostemma githago subsp. thessalum include its potential to reduce fever, relieve coughs and colds, and support digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to note that its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to potential side effects and interactions.
